behaviors.description: A cell containing `=SUM(A1:A10)` evaluates to the correct sum of the values in cells A1 through A10.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: range reference parsing (`A1:A10`), cell-value resolution across multiple cells, and SUM function execution through the formula engine path. Hypothetical elsewhere: persistence, undo/redo, cross-sheet logic. MOCK-MISS: real failure can occur at parser/resolver/function-module handoff, not just inside a single arithmetic function. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none required. Rejection-gate: rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is purely deterministic in-process formula evaluation with no OS/network/model dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create an in-memory workbook, set A1..A10 numeric values, evaluate `=SUM(A1:A10)` in another cell via the normal engine API, and assert the computed result equals the expected total.

behaviors.description: A cell referencing another cell that contains a formula (e.g., `=B1+1` where B1 is `=A1*2`) produces the correct cascaded result.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: dependency chaining across cells where one formula consumes another formula’s result. Hypothetical elsewhere: disk format and history stack. MOCK-MISS: single-function unit tests miss failures in dependency graph propagation between modules. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not present. Rejection-gate: rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text only requires deterministic local recalculation.
behaviors.test_description: In an in-memory workbook, set `A1=3`, `B1==A1*2`, `C1==B1+1`; evaluate C1 through the engine and assert `C1==7` and intermediate dependency evaluation succeeds.

behaviors.description: The engine detects a circular dependency (A1 references B1, B1 references A1) and returns a circular reference error instead of looping.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: cycle detection across dependency graph nodes and error propagation to cell result state. Hypothetical elsewhere: serialization and UI reporting. MOCK-MISS: isolated unit of a DFS helper can pass while integration between graph build and evaluator fails. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: rejected System/Agentic/Workflow since no real OS/network/model dependency is specified.
behaviors.test_description: Set `A1==B1+1` and `B1==A1+1` in a workbook; run evaluation and assert a circular-reference error is returned (and evaluation terminates).

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP with an exact match returns the correct value from the lookup table, and returns an error when the key is not found.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: built-in function dispatch, table/range access, exact-match lookup semantics, and not-found error propagation. Hypothetical elsewhere: cross-sheet and persistence behaviors. MOCK-MISS: direct unit test of lookup algorithm can miss engine-level argument/range wiring issues. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because this is deterministic local function evaluation.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a lookup table range in-sheet, evaluate `VLOOKUP` exact-match formula once with an existing key and once with a missing key through engine evaluation API; assert returned value and error code respectively.

behaviors.description: The IF function correctly handles nested conditions (e.g., `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")`).
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: deterministic nested conditional expression semantics. Hypothetical elsewhere: dependency invalidation, persistence, undo/redo. MOCK-MISS: failure is primarily expression logic/branching, reproducible within one evaluator boundary without multi-module wiring. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: absent. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because behavior text does not require module handoff beyond single expression evaluation object.
behaviors.test_description: Call expression evaluator on the nested IF formula with controlled A1 values (e.g., 5, 15, 25) and assert outputs `low`, `medium`, `high` respectively.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins cell values with the correct separator and handles empty cells by skipping them rather than inserting blank strings.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: string concatenation semantics with empty-value handling in one function contract. Hypothetical elsewhere: sheet resolution, storage format, undo stack. MOCK-MISS: core failure is local function logic; no explicit module boundary is required by behavior text. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because behavior does not explicitly require cross-module state propagation.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke CONCATENATE evaluation with a controlled argument list containing non-empty and empty cell values plus separator, asserting output omits empty entries and separator placement matches spec.

behaviors.description: A formula referencing a cell in a different sheet (e.g., `=Sheet2!A1+1`) resolves correctly when the referenced sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: cross-sheet name resolution and value fetch across workbook modules. Hypothetical elsewhere: missing-sheet error behavior details, persistence. MOCK-MISS: unit tests of arithmetic or parser alone miss sheet-resolution boundary failures. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not required. Rejection-gate: rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because no external infra or model dependency is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with `Sheet2!A1=10`, evaluate `=Sheet2!A1+1` on another sheet through engine API, assert result is 11.

behaviors.description: When a referenced cell's value changes, all dependent formulas are recalculated in the correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: dependency graph invalidation plus ordered recomputation across multiple formula nodes. Hypothetical elsewhere: external I/O or model scoring. MOCK-MISS: isolated formula unit tests miss orchestration failures in recalculation scheduler. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text specifies logical recalculation order, not real OS/network effects.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a dependency chain/diamond (e.g., A1 -> B1/C1 -> D1), mutate A1, trigger recomputation, and assert final values match expected propagation order effects.

behaviors.description: The undo operation after editing a formula restores both the formula text and all previously computed dependent values.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: interaction between edit command/history stack and recalculation engine state rollback. Hypothetical elsewhere: file persistence and binary format. MOCK-MISS: unit tests of undo stack alone miss coupling failures with dependent-value restoration. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior is deterministic in-process state restoration.
behaviors.test_description: Edit a formula that changes downstream cells, verify new values, execute undo, then assert original formula text and all dependent computed values are fully restored.

behaviors.description: Saving a workbook to disk and loading it back produces identical cell values and formulas — no data loss or precision change in floating-point results.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: serialization/deserialization fidelity and floating-point preservation across save/load path. Hypothetical elsewhere: file watcher events, networked storage, subprocess tooling. REAL-INFRA check: although text says disk, failure target is data fidelity of serializer/parser; this can be caught with local file/in-process I/O and does not require OS-specific event/timing behavior. MOCK-HIDE: pure mock serializer would hide defects, so test must exercise real codec path. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: rejected Agentic/Workflow due no AI dependence; rejected System because behavior text does not require subprocess/network/OS primitives beyond ordinary local persistence.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with formulas and representative floating-point values, save to a temp file via production binary serializer, reload via production loader, and assert formulas and numeric values are equal within strict precision policy.
